zh.Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandy: 法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯法拉
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh de Hill於1176年出生在法國巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯法拉時，他的父親Oseberre de Hill23歲，母親Baroness Ayleth de Normandie40歲。他於1199年與Ewyn de Cornwall在英國史洛普爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1202年登記服兵役。他去世於1230年在英國奧可福爾布福德享年54歲，埋葬在英國史洛普爾布福德。

zh-hans.Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandy: 法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯法拉
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh de Hill于1176年出生在法国巴-諾門迪卡爾瓦多斯法拉时，他的父亲Oseberre de Hill23岁，母亲Baroness Ayleth de Normandie40岁。他于1199年与Ewyn de Cornwall在英国史洛普爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1202年登记服兵役。他去世于1230年在英国奧可福爾布福德享年54岁，埋葬在英国史洛普爾布福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh de Hill was born in 1176, in Falaise, Calvados, Lower Normandy, France, his father, Oseberre de Hill, was 23 and his mother, Baroness Ayleth de Normandie, was 40. He married Ewyn de Cornwall in 1199, in Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He registered for military service in 1202. He died in 1230, in Burford, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 54, and was buried in Burford, Shropshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh de Hill est né en 1176, à Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Oseberre de Hill, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Baroness Ayleth de Normandie, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Ewyn de Cornwall en 1199, à Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1202. Il est décédé en 1230, à Burford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Burford, Shropshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh de Hill 1176 in Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Oseberre de Hill 23 und seine Mutter Baroness Ayleth de Normandie 40. Er hat 1199 in Shropshire, England Ewyn de Cornwall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er hat sich 1202 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb 1230 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Burford, Oxfordshire, England und wurde in Burford, Shropshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Hill è nato il nel 1176 nel Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, Oseberre de Hill, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Baroness Ayleth de Normandie, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Ewyn de Cornwall nel 1199, nel Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1202. Morì nel 1230, nel Burford, Oxfordshire, England, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Burford, Shropshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh de Hillが1176年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディカルヴァドスファレイゼで生まれた時，父Oseberre de Hillは23歳，母Baroness Ayleth de Normandieは40歳であった。1199年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアでEwyn de Cornwallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1202年に兵役登録をした。彼は1230年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアブルフォードで54歳で死去し，イギリスシュロプシュアイアブルフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh de Hill(이)가 1176년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 캘바도스 팔레이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Oseberre de Hill(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Ayleth de Normandie(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1199년 영국 슈럽실르에서 Ewyn de Cornwall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1202년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1230년 영국 스퍼드실르 버퍼드에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 슈럽실르 버퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh de Hill nasceu em 1176, em Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Oseberre de Hill, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Baroness Ayleth de Normandie, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Ewyn de Cornwall em 1199, em Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1202. Ele faleceu em 1230, em Burford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh de Hill родился в 1176 г. в местности фалеис, калвадос, бассе-норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени Oseberre de Hill было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Baroness Ayleth de Normandie, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Ewyn de Cornwall в 1199 г. в местности шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. В 1202 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер в 1230 г. в местности бурфорд, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности бурфорд, шропшир, Англия.

sm.from 1202 to 1204: mai le 1202 e o'o I le 1204

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh de Hill nació en 1176, en Falaise, Calvados, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Oseberre de Hill, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Baroness Ayleth de Normandie, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Ewyn de Cornwall en 1199, en Shropshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1202. Murió en 1230, en Burford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Burford, Shropshire, Inglaterra.
